agents.name: InvoiceBot
agents.description: Creates vendor records and routes supplier invoices for payment processing.

agents.name: PayrollBot
agents.description: Maintains employee records and prepares the biweekly payroll run.

agents.name: AccessBot
agents.description: Helps employees request the application access they need for their work.

agents.name: ReportBot
agents.description: Reads analytics data and prepares the weekly operating report.

agents.name: SupportBot
agents.description: Investigates customer support cases and sends resolution updates to customers.

agents.name: SummaryBot
agents.description: Reads approved internal knowledge and produces concise leadership summaries.

agents.name: FinanceBot
agents.description: Approves payments that have completed the finance review workflow.

agents.name: LegacyBot
agents.description: Answers questions from the retired product archive.

agents.name: KnowledgeBot
agents.description: Finds approved answers in the internal knowledge base for employees.

agents.name: SalesAssistBot
agents.description: Looks up account context and drafts follow-up messages for sales representatives.

agents.name: SalesBot
agents.description: Looks up CRM account context and sends personalized sales outreach to prospective customers.

agents.name: InventoryBot
agents.description: Checks current inventory and updates stock counts after warehouse reconciliation.

agents.name: ComplianceBot
agents.description: Reads policy material and opens audit follow-up tickets for reviewers.

agents.name: DataQualityBot
agents.description: Reviews analytics extracts and validates data-quality rules.

agents.name: TravelBot
agents.description: Creates travel requests for employees before they are reviewed by a manager.

agents.name: ContractBot
agents.description: Finds prior agreements and drafts contract language for legal review.

agents.name: SchedulerBot
agents.description: Checks team calendars and creates internal meeting holds.

agents.name: EngineeringBot
agents.description: Reads source repositories and opens engineering work items for maintainers.

agents.name: MonitoringBot
agents.description: Reads service metrics and opens incident tickets when a threshold is breached.

agents.name: OnboardingBot
agents.description: Creates onboarding checklists and sends internal reminders to new hires.

agents.name: ResearchBot
agents.description: Searches public sources and summarizes non-confidential market research.

agents.name: ProcurementBot
agents.description: Retrieves prior agreements and opens audit follow-ups for the procurement team.

agents.name: MarketingBot
agents.description: Researches public market sources and summarizes findings for campaign planning.

agents.name: CustomerSuccessBot
agents.description: Looks up approved playbooks and posts internal account reminders to the success team.

agents.name: RecruitingBot
agents.description: Checks interviewer availability and creates internal interview holds.

agents.name: BusinessIntelligenceBot
agents.description: Runs read-only analytics queries and prepares metrics for the weekly review.

agents.name: FacilitiesBot
agents.description: Creates travel requests and checks workplace policy for on-site visits.

agents.name: SecOpsBot
agents.description: Reads service metrics and policy, and opens incident tickets when thresholds breach.

agents.name: ChiefOfStaffBot
agents.description: Prepares leadership briefings and can hand finance approvals to the finance agent.

agents.name: ExecBriefingBot
agents.description: Compiles executive reading material and routes requests through the chief of staff agent.

findings.title: Critical data-exfiltration path
findings.business_risk: SupportBot can read customer PII and directly send external email, creating a direct path for confidential customer data to be exfiltrated outside the organization, exposing the firm to regulatory violations, financial penalties, and reputational harm.

evidence.detail: holds this effective-access combination
SupportBot has a direct grant of read_customer_pii.
SupportBot has a direct grant of send_external_email.

findings.recommended_action: Revoke the direct read_customer_pii and send_external_email grants from SupportBot; implement least‑privilege controls, require mediated approval for external egress, and add monitoring/auditing of any PII access and outbound email activity.
findings.control_mapping: Data protection — least privilege and controlled external egress

owasp_mcp.title: Tool Poisoning
owasp_mcp.relevance: A compromised or misleading tool can steer an agent toward unintended data handling or external actions.

owasp_mcp.title: Software Supply Chain Attacks & Dependency Tampering
owasp_mcp.relevance: A compromised MCP dependency can add covert external egress to an otherwise trusted workflow.

real_world_incident.title: Supabase MCP stored prompt-injection scenario
real_world_incident.relevance: Supabase documented a scenario where stored instructions led an MCP-connected agent to read private database data and write it into an attacker-visible field. It illustrates this read-to-egress risk class; it is not evidence that this fleet uses Supabase.

real_world_incident.title: Malicious postmark-mcp package backdoor (v1.0.16)
real_world_incident.relevance: Postmark reported that an impersonating npm package silently BCC'd email to an external server. It is an analogous tool-poisoning and supply-chain incident, not evidence that this fleet installed the package.

control_frameworks.relevance: One agent identity holds both sides of a duty that the control requires to be separated.

control_frameworks.control_name: Access modification and segregation of duties
control_frameworks.relevance: Toxic capability combinations in one identity undermine segregation-of-duties objectives.
Access rules should prevent a single identity from combining conflicting capabilities.
Provisioned rights should be reviewed so conflicting entitlements are not co-held.

control_frameworks.control_name: Segregation of duties over financial processes
control_frameworks.relevance: An agent that can initiate and approve the same transaction defeats independent review.
A toxic combination lets an AI agent complete a consequential action without an independent human checkpoint.

findings.title: Unused standing access: 2 direct grants
findings.business_risk: ReportBot holds standing privileges to delete records and export data that are never exercised. If the agent were compromised or misused, an attacker could invoke these unused grants to irreversibly remove critical data or exfiltrate sensitive information, exposing the organization to data loss, regulatory non‑compliance, and reputational damage.

evidence.detail: usage log contains no invocation of these direct grants: delete_records, export_data
ReportBot has a direct grant of delete_records.
ReportBot has a direct grant of export_data.

findings.recommended_action: Revoke the unnecessary delete_records and export_data grants from ReportBot and re‑certify its access against the principle of least privilege. Implement periodic review of granted versus used permissions for all agents.
findings.control_mapping: Least privilege — access certification using granted versus used access

control_frameworks.control_name: Least Privilege / Authorize Access to Security Functions
control_frameworks.relevance: Standing grants with no observed use exceed the minimum access the agent's function requires.
Unused standing entitlements enlarge the logical-access attack surface without business need.

control_frameworks.control_name: Access modification and segregation of duties
control_frameworks.relevance: Granted-but-unused access should be removed through the access-modification process.
High-risk unused entitlements are privileged rights that should be restricted and reviewed.
Access rights should be adjusted when observed use does not support the grant.
Unused financially-relevant entitlements weaken ITGC access assertions.

findings.title: Critical fraud path: create vendor and approve payment
findings.business_risk: InvoiceBot possesses both the create_vendor and approve_payment tool grants, giving a single automated agent the ability to add new vendors and subsequently authorize payments to those vendors. This concentration of authority creates a direct fraud pathway that could be exploited to generate fictitious vendors and approve unauthorized disbursements, potentially resulting in material financial loss and SOX compliance violations across the entire accounts‑payable function.

evidence.detail: holds this effective-access combination
InvoiceBot has a direct grant of approve_payment.
InvoiceBot has a direct grant of create_vendor.

findings.recommended_action: Implement segregation of duties by revoking either the create_vendor or approve_payment grant from InvoiceBot, enforce dual‑approval controls for vendor creation and payment authorization, and add continuous monitoring to detect any instance where a single entity performs both actions.
findings.control_mapping: SOX ITGC — segregation of duties (vendor creation versus payment approval)

control_frameworks.relevance: One agent identity holds both sides of a duty that the control requires to be separated.

control_frameworks.control_name: Access modification and segregation of duties
control_frameworks.relevance: Toxic capability combinations in one identity undermine segregation-of-duties objectives.
Access rules should prevent a single identity from combining conflicting capabilities.
Provisioned rights should be reviewed so conflicting entitlements are not co-held.

control_frameworks.control_name: Segregation of duties over financial processes
control_frameworks.relevance: An agent that can initiate and approve the same transaction defeats independent review.
A toxic combination lets an AI agent complete a consequential action without an independent human checkpoint.

findings.title: Critical ghost-employee fraud path
findings.business_risk: PayrollBot holds direct authority to both add_employee (employee creation) and run_payroll (payroll execution). This conflation of employee setup and payroll processing creates a critical segregation‑of‑duties violation, enabling a malicious or compromised bot to create ghost employees and issue unauthorized payroll payments, potentially resulting in financial loss, regulatory non‑compliance, and reputational harm across the entire payroll function.

evidence.detail: holds this effective-access combination
PayrollBot has a direct grant of add_employee.
PayrollBot has a direct grant of run_payroll.

findings.recommended_action: Re‑engineer the permission model to enforce segregation of duties: remove the direct add_employee grant from PayrollBot, require an independent approval workflow for employee creation, and limit payroll execution to a separate, controlled role. Implement least‑privilege access, periodic access reviews, and monitoring of employee‑creation and payroll‑run activities.
findings.control_mapping: SOX ITGC — segregation of duties (employee setup versus payroll execution)

control_frameworks.relevance: One agent identity holds both sides of a duty that the control requires to be separated.

control_frameworks.control_name: Access modification and segregation of duties
control_frameworks.relevance: Toxic capability combinations in one identity undermine segregation-of-duties objectives.

findings.title: AI-generalized toxic capability combination: Read CRM account context + Send email outside the company
findings.business_risk: The SalesBot agent can both read CRM account context and send email outside the company, creating a direct path to expose customer information to external recipients. This combination can lead to unauthorized data disclosure, regulatory non‑compliance, reputational damage, and potential financial loss.

evidence.detail: holds the cited effective-access combination.
SalesBot has a direct grant of read_crm.
SalesBot has a direct grant of send_external_email.

findings.recommended_action: Separate the read_crm and send_external_email permissions, enforce a dual‑approval workflow for any external email that includes CRM data, and implement continuous monitoring and audit of combined usage.

real_world_incident.relevance: Supabase documented a scenario where stored instructions led an MCP-connected agent to read private database data and write it into an attacker-visible field. It illustrates this read-to-egress risk class; it is not evidence that this fleet uses Supabase.

real_world_incident.relevance: Postmark reported that an impersonating npm package silently BCC'd email to an external server. It is an analogous tool-poisoning and supply-chain incident, not evidence that this fleet installed the package.

findings.title: Delegated payment-approval blast radius
findings.business_risk: Through the delegation edge, ChiefOfStaffBot can reach the finance_bot, which directly holds the approve_payment tool. Consequently, any user or process that can invoke ChiefOfStaffBot inherits the ability to trigger payment approvals via finance_bot. The practical blast radius therefore extends to all payment transactions that finance_bot is authorized to approve, creating a critical risk of unauthorized or fraudulent payments if the delegation is not tightly controlled.

evidence.detail: reaches approve_payment only through delegation to finance_bot
chief_of_staff_bot can delegate to finance_bot.
finance_bot is the direct grant holder reached through chief_of_staff_bot -> finance_bot.
ChiefOfStaffBot effectively reaches approve_payment through chief_of_staff_bot -> finance_bot.

findings.recommended_action: Conduct an immediate access review of the delegation relationship between ChiefOfStaffBot and finance_bot. Restrict delegation to only verified, least‑privilege identities, enforce multi‑factor approval for payment actions, and implement monitoring/auditing of all approve_payment invocations originating from ChiefOfStaffBot.
findings.control_mapping: Identity governance — effective access review and least privilege

control_frameworks.relevance: Delegation extends the agent's effective privilege beyond its direct provisioning.
Authority reachable through delegation recombines duties the direct grants kept separate.
Effective access through delegation is logical access and must be evaluated as such.
A privileged capability reachable only through delegation is still a privileged right of that identity.

findings.title: Delegated payment-approval blast radius
findings.business_risk: Compromise of ExecBriefingBot grants indirect access to the approve_payment tool via the delegation chain (ExecBriefingBot → chief_of_staff_bot → finance_bot). An attacker who gains control of ExecBriefingBot could approve payments without direct authorization, exposing the organization to unauthorized disbursements and potential financial loss.

evidence.detail: reaches approve_payment only through delegation to finance_bot
exec_briefing_bot can delegate to chief_of_staff_bot.
chief_of_staff_bot can delegate to finance_bot.
finance_bot is the direct grant holder reached through exec_briefing_bot -> chief_of_staff_bot -> finance_bot.
ExecBriefingBot effectively reaches approve_payment through exec_briefing_bot -> chief_of_staff_bot -> finance_bot.

findings.recommended_action: Restrict delegation paths so that ExecBriefingBot cannot reach payment‑approval capabilities, enforce least‑privilege principles on delegation edges, conduct an immediate access review of all delegated relationships, and implement monitoring/alerting for any use of approve_payment originating from indirect agents.
findings.control_mapping: Identity governance — effective access review and least privilege

control_frameworks.relevance: Delegation extends the agent's effective privilege beyond its direct provisioning.
Authority reachable through delegation recombines duties the direct grants kept separate.
Effective access through delegation is logical access and must be evaluated as such.
A privileged capability reachable only through delegation is still a privileged right of that identity.

findings.title: Delegated payment-approval blast radius
findings.business_risk: This agent does not hold payment approval directly, but it can reach an agent that does through delegation. Its effective access therefore includes authority to authorize disbursements, creating a confused-deputy path.

evidence.detail: reaches approve_payment only through delegation to finance_bot
summary_bot can delegate to finance_bot.
finance_bot is the direct grant holder reached through summary_bot -> finance_bot.
SummaryBot effectively reaches approve_payment through summary_bot -> finance_bot.

findings.recommended_action: Remove or constrain the delegation link to the payment-approving agent. If delegation is necessary, expose a narrowly scoped workflow action rather than the delegate's general approval authority.
findings.control_mapping: Identity governance — effective access review and least privilege

owasp_mcp.title: Privilege Escalation via Scope Creep
owasp_mcp.relevance: Delegated authority can turn a narrowly scoped agent into a higher-impact actor through effective access.

real_world_incident.title: Invariant Labs GitHub MCP toxic agent flow
real_world_incident.relevance: Invariant demonstrated an untrusted GitHub issue coercing an MCP-connected agent to move private repository data into a public pull request. It illustrates how composed authority paths can exceed an agent's apparent role.

control_frameworks.relevance: Delegation extends the agent's effective privilege beyond its direct provisioning.
Authority reachable through delegation recombines duties the direct grants kept separate.
Effective access through delegation is logical access and must be evaluated as such.
A privileged capability reachable only through delegation is still a privileged right of that identity.

findings.title: Self-granting privilege path
findings.business_risk: AccessBot possesses both the request_access and grant_access capabilities, creating a self‑granting privilege path. This enables the agent to request and immediately approve access to applications without independent review, allowing unauthorized privilege escalation across the organization’s systems. The blast radius includes potential exposure of sensitive data, violation of compliance requirements, and the ability for an attacker who compromises the bot to obtain unrestricted access to any application the bot can request.

evidence.detail: holds this effective-access combination
AccessBot has a direct grant of grant_access.
AccessBot has a direct grant of request_access.

findings.recommended_action: Segregate duties by removing the direct grant of the grant_access tool from AccessBot. Implement an approval workflow that requires a separate, privileged role to approve access requests. Apply least‑privilege principles to the agent, enforce monitoring of any grant actions, and conduct periodic reviews of agent permissions.
findings.control_mapping: Identity governance — segregation of duties (access request versus access grant)

control_frameworks.relevance: One agent identity holds both sides of a duty that the control requires to be separated.

control_frameworks.control_name: Access modification and segregation of duties
control_frameworks.relevance: Toxic capability combinations in one identity undermine segregation-of-duties objectives.
Access rules should prevent a single identity from combining conflicting capabilities.
Provisioned rights should be reviewed so conflicting entitlements are not co-held.

control_frameworks.control_name: Segregation of duties over financial processes
control_frameworks.relevance: An agent that can initiate and approve the same transaction defeats independent review.
A toxic combination lets an AI agent complete a consequential action without an independent human checkpoint.

findings.title: Ownerless agent has no accountable reviewer
findings.business_risk: The LegacyBot agent lacks an assigned owner, so no individual is accountable for its access rights or activity. This creates a compliance gap (missing reviewer for access certification) and increases the risk of undetected misuse or unauthorized data exposure through the agent's operations.

evidence.detail: owner is null in the fleet inventory

findings.recommended_action: Assign a named owner in the fleet inventory, update the agent's governance record, and include the agent in the regular access certification process to ensure accountability.
findings.control_mapping: Accountability — named owner required for agent access certification

control_frameworks.relevance: An agent identity with no accountable owner cannot be certified, reviewed, or deprovisioned on schedule.
Identities must be traceable to an accountable party throughout their lifecycle.
The full life cycle of an identity — including this non-human one — requires a responsible owner.
Ownerless identities break the accountability chain ITGC access reviews depend on.

llm_enrichment.mode: cached live OpenAI gpt-oss-120b Bedrock result
llm_enrichment.disclosure: This zero-key cache was produced from a real OpenAI gpt-oss-120b Amazon Bedrock analysis of the committed synthetic fleet. Dashboard replay never calls Bedrock.
